citation_short: HLC/HLC Kosovo, History and Methodology of the Kosovo Memory Book (2015)
title: History and Methodology of the Kosovo Memory Book
author_or_org: Humanitarian Law Center; Humanitarian Law Center Kosovo
geographic_scope: Kosovo; NATO bombing victims in Kosovo/Serbia/Montenegro
strengths: Near-comprehensive named-victim registry; audited by HRDAG; provides civilian/military status splits and bombing-victim sub-series.
limitations: High-level public tables emphasize 'killed or disappeared' rather than direct-vs-disappearance coding; perpetrator coding is incomplete at summary level.
notes: Best conflict-wide context source, but not always sufficient by itself for core direct-death DR input.

citation_short: HLC, Human losses during the NATO bombing (2012)
title: Human losses during the NATO bombing
strengths: Provides Kosovo-internal splits for NATO-caused civilian and military deaths and detailed side-attributed military counts.
limitations: Some headline series mix killed, died, and forcibly disappeared; non-Albanian civilian counts differ from later HLC summaries.
notes: Used for NATO-caused deaths inside Kosovo and for a best-estimate military total.

citation_short: HLC, On the occasion of the state commemoration of the NATO bombing anniversary (2023)
title: On the occasion of the state commemoration of the NATO bombing anniversary
strengths: Explicit attacker/victim-side direct-killing claims for Serbian-force, KLA, and NATO-related sub-series; newer than the 2012 note.
limitations: Not all geographic qualifiers are restated in detail; some figures differ from the 2012 HLC summary and must be handled as an update or alternative coding state.
notes: Used for attacker-specific direct-killing components and as a high-bound scenario.

citation_short: HLC, The cover-up of evidence of crimes during the war in Kosovo (2017)
title: The cover-up of evidence of crimes during the war in Kosovo: The concealment of bodies operation
strengths: Gives a stark comparable civilian-killing benchmark for 1998 versus the first week after NATO intervention; links killings to mass-grave evidence.
limitations: Focused on Kosovo Albanian civilians and concealment operation, not whole-episode civilian/military totals.
notes: Used mainly to justify episode splitting and the change in violence intensity after 24 March 1999.

citation_short: HRW, Under Orders: War Crimes in Kosovo (2001)
title: UNDER ORDERS: War Crimes in Kosovo - 4. March-June 1999: An Overview
strengths: Provides widely used displacement totals and a lower population anchor used in conflict-era reporting.
limitations: Population figures are approximate; not designed as a denominator study for DR.
notes: Used for refugee-outflow and low-bound civilian-exposure scenarios.

citation_short: ICTY, Milutinović et al. Trial Judgment, vol. I (2009)
title: Prosecutor v. Milutinović et al., Trial Judgment, Volume I
author_or_org: International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia
strengths: Detailed force-structure and strength discussion under oath with documentary citations.
limitations: Not a purpose-built monthly deployment dataset; some figures are complement ceilings or include re-subordinated units.
notes: Used to build military person-month scenarios without resorting to whole-country force totals.

citation_short: Brunborg, Demographic and war-loss report for ICTY OTP (2002)
title: Milosevic_Kosovo_020814
strengths: Critically assesses competing Kosovo population estimates and distinguishes de jure from de facto logic.
limitations: Not a daily wartime presence series; 1998 estimates must still be carried forward into 1999 with scenario assumptions.

citation_short: HIWG/Refworld, Meeting Humanitarian Needs in Kosovo Province (1998)
title: Meeting Humanitarian Needs in Kosovo Province of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia
date_scope: up to 1998-10-13; update written in November 1998
strengths: Good contemporaneous benchmark for 1998 internal and external displacement before the ceasefire/verification period.
limitations: Only a snapshot; not enough to build a full pre-NATO person-month denominator.
notes: Used to justify that the pre-NATO episode already had major displacement and should not be merged mechanically with the bombing window.

title: Kosovo Conflict Chronology: September 1998 - March 1999
strengths: Convenient citation to a UNHCR return/displacement snapshot after the October 1998 accord.
limitations: Secondary relay of UNHCR figures; single date only.
